texts.text: TAKE NOTICE that after thirty (30) days from the date of this Gazette, and unless cause be shown to the contrary, I intend to apply to the High Court  at  Nairobi    for  representation  of  the  estates  of  the  persons  named  in  the  second  column  of  the  schedule  hereto,  who  died  on  the  dates respectively set forth against their names.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: And further take notice that all persons having any claims against or interests in the estates of the said deceased persons are required to prove such claims or interests before me within two (2) months from the date of this Gazette, after which date the claims and interests are proved will be paid and satisfied and the several estates distributed according to law.
texts.text: And further take notice that all persons having any claims against or interests in the estates of the said deceased persons are required to prove such claims or interests before me within two (2) months from the date of this Gazette, after which date the claims and interests are proved will be paid and satisfied and the several estates distributed according to law.

texts.text: NOTICE  is  given  that  the  Deposit  Protection  Fund  Board,  in exercise of the powers under rule 107 of the Companies (Winding-up) rules,  shall  with  effect  from  14th  December,  2009,  appoint  the liquidation  agent  of  Meridien  Biao  Bank  Limited  (in  liquidation)  to pay a second dividend to all depositors and creditors who have proved their debts.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Payments will be made during working days (Monday to Friday) at  the  institution's  offices  on  the  2nd  Floor  of  Marshalls  House, Harambee Avenue, Nairobi, between the hours of 9.00 a.m. and 4.00 p.m.
texts.text: Payments will be made during working days (Monday to Friday) at  the  institution's  offices  on  the  2nd  Floor  of  Marshalls  House, Harambee Avenue, Nairobi, between the hours of 9.00 a.m. and 4.00 p.m.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Claims  for  the  second  dividend  should  be  made  within  a  period not exceeding one (1) year effective 14th December, 2009. Depositors and other creditors who have already lodged their claims automatically  qualify  for  the  second  dividend  and  need  not  claim again.

texts.text: ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT ASSESSMENT STUDY REPORT FOR THE PROPOSED SEWER LINE DIVERSION OF THE EPZA SEWER LINE LOCATED BETWEEN PLOT NUMBERS: LR. 337/976, LR.337/1074 AND LR 337/2719 IN ATHI RIVER

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PURSUANT to regulation 21 of Environmental (Impact Assessment and Audit) Regulations, the National Environment Management  Authority  (NEMA)  has  received  an  Environmental Impact  Assessment  Study  Report  for  the  implementation  of  the Proposed Sewer Line Diversion of the Epza Sewer Line.
texts.text: PURSUANT to regulation 21 of Environmental (Impact Assessment and Audit) Regulations, the National Environment Management  Authority  (NEMA)  has  received  an  Environmental Impact  Assessment  Study  Report  for  the  implementation  of  the Proposed Sewer Line Diversion of the Epza Sewer Line.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The proponent intends to create a sewage diversion of around 110 meters  for  its  factory  expansion  project  on  No.  LR.  337/2719.  The diversion  is  to  provide  space  for  the  factory  expansion.  The  site  is located  in  Athi  River  in  Mavoko  Municipality.  The  site  is  located between Plot Numbers: 337/976, 337/1074 337/2719.
texts.text: The proponent intends to create a sewage diversion of around 110 meters  for  its  factory  expansion  project  on  No.  LR.  337/2719.  The diversion  is  to  provide  space  for  the  factory  expansion.  The  site  is located  in  Athi  River  in  Mavoko  Municipality.  The  site  is  located between Plot Numbers: 337/976, 337/1074 337/2719.

texts.orig: The  project  anticipates  the  following  impacts  and  mitigation measures:
texts.text: The  project  anticipates  the  following  impacts  and  mitigation measures:

texts.label: list_item

texts.label: list_item

texts.text:  Acceptable  grading  as  per  the  consultants expert should be provided

texts.orig:  If  rock is present it should be removed to a depth of 4 to 6 inches below the bottom of the pipes' placement. if the soil is soft it will have to be stabilized with a packed layer of gravel or broken rock
texts.text:  If  rock is present it should be removed to a depth of 4 to 6 inches below the bottom of the pipes' placement. if the soil is soft it will have to be stabilized with a packed layer of gravel or broken rock

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Frequently the trench will collect water especially because we are in the rainy season
texts.text:  Frequently the trench will collect water especially because we are in the rainy season

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  sewer  will  have  to  be  layed  from  the lowest part of the line to permit the water to flow away  from the completed sections centrifugal  pumps  will  be  required    to  be used for dewatering the trench.
texts.text:  The  sewer  will  have  to  be  layed  from  the lowest part of the line to permit the water to flow away  from the completed sections centrifugal  pumps  will  be  required    to  be used for dewatering the trench.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  For muddy material a diaphragm pump will be required.
texts.text:  For muddy material a diaphragm pump will be required.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  For wet sections well points will be used or a  drain  with  open  joints    and  bedded  with gravel built underneath the sewer to remove the water.
texts.text:  For wet sections well points will be used or a  drain  with  open  joints    and  bedded  with gravel built underneath the sewer to remove the water.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The concrete pipes will have to be placed in their  exact  positions,  the  elevation  of  the pipes  will  be  checked  and  if  correct  a  joint will be made.
texts.text:  The concrete pipes will have to be placed in their  exact  positions,  the  elevation  of  the pipes  will  be  checked  and  if  correct  a  joint will be made.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Where an immediate connection is not made,  a  vitrified  -  clay  will  have  to  be connected  to the branch  bell to prevent entrance of dirt or ground water.
texts.text:  Where an immediate connection is not made,  a  vitrified  -  clay  will  have  to  be connected  to the branch  bell to prevent entrance of dirt or ground water.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Branches  or  slants,  for  factory  connections must be installed at the proper locations and with the stubs pointing to the correct side of the location.
texts.text:  Branches  or  slants,  for  factory  connections must be installed at the proper locations and with the stubs pointing to the correct side of the location.

texts.label: list_item

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Because of the current construction activities  in  the  area,  the  need  for  a  firm backfill  will  be  required  because  of  the traffic issue
texts.text:  Because of the current construction activities  in  the  area,  the  need  for  a  firm backfill  will  be  required  because  of  the traffic issue

texts.orig: ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT ASSESSMENT STUDY REPORT FOR THE PROPOSED NATIONAL CO-OPERATIVE HOUSING UNION (NACHU) PRESTIGE HOUSING SCHEME ON L.R. No. 23405 MAVOKO MUNICIPAL COUNCIL
texts.text: ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT ASSESSMENT STUDY REPORT FOR THE PROPOSED NATIONAL CO-OPERATIVE HOUSING UNION (NACHU) PRESTIGE HOUSING SCHEME ON L.R. No. 23405 MAVOKO MUNICIPAL COUNCIL

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PURSUANT to regulation 21 of environmental (Impact Assessment and Audit) Regulations, the National Environment Management  Authority  (NEMA)  has  received  an  Environmental Impact  Assessment  Study  Report  for  the  implementation  of  the proposed  National  Co-operative  Housing  Union  (NACHU)  prestige Housing Scheme.
texts.text: PURSUANT to regulation 21 of environmental (Impact Assessment and Audit) Regulations, the National Environment Management  Authority  (NEMA)  has  received  an  Environmental Impact  Assessment  Study  Report  for  the  implementation  of  the proposed  National  Co-operative  Housing  Union  (NACHU)  prestige Housing Scheme.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  proposed  project  will  comprise  of  Type  A  semi  detached luxury  masionettes  housed on 70 No. plots of  minimum size 21.0 x 8.5m, each maisonnette is to have a plinth area of 100m². The second proposal will have Type B and will be composed of flats planned and arranged in 10 No. Each block is to accommodate 16 No. flats with a plinth area of 102.0m² per flat. The proposed project will be located on L.R. No. 23405 Mavoko Municipal Council.
texts.text: The  proposed  project  will  comprise  of  Type  A  semi  detached luxury  masionettes  housed on 70 No. plots of  minimum size 21.0 x 8.5m, each maisonnette is to have a plinth area of 100m². The second proposal will have Type B and will be composed of flats planned and arranged in 10 No. Each block is to accommodate 16 No. flats with a plinth area of 102.0m² per flat. The proposed project will be located on L.R. No. 23405 Mavoko Municipal Council.

texts.text: ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT ASSESSMENT STUDY REPORT FOR THE PROPOSED DEVELOPMENT OF A HOUSING ESTATE ON L.R. NO. 10426 /36, 37, 38 & 39, MAVOKO MUNICIPALITY, MACHAKOS DISTRICT

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PURSUANT to regulation 21 of Environmental (Impact Assessment and Audit) Regulations, the National Environment Management  Authority  (NEMA)  has  received  an  Environmental Impact  Assessment  Study  Report  for  the  implementation  of  the Proposed Development of a Housing Estate on L.R. No. 10426 /36, 37, 38 & 39, Mavoko Municipality.
texts.text: PURSUANT to regulation 21 of Environmental (Impact Assessment and Audit) Regulations, the National Environment Management  Authority  (NEMA)  has  received  an  Environmental Impact  Assessment  Study  Report  for  the  implementation  of  the Proposed Development of a Housing Estate on L.R. No. 10426 /36, 37, 38 & 39, Mavoko Municipality.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  proponent  intends  to  put  up  a  housing  estate  comprising  a total  of  262  low  cost  bungalows  of  two  and  three  bedrooms,  3  No. Plots have been set aside for sale to commercial developers (to bring commercial outlets closer to the estate) using regulated designs while 2 No. Plots have been set aside for development of a kindergarten.
texts.text: The  proponent  intends  to  put  up  a  housing  estate  comprising  a total  of  262  low  cost  bungalows  of  two  and  three  bedrooms,  3  No. Plots have been set aside for sale to commercial developers (to bring commercial outlets closer to the estate) using regulated designs while 2 No. Plots have been set aside for development of a kindergarten.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The proposed development site comprises four plots registered as L.R.  Nos.  10426/36,  37,  38  &  39  and  is  located  outside  Athi  River town in Machakos District. The site lies about 0.7 km off Mombasa Road, opposite Athi River Steel Plant Limited.
texts.text: The proposed development site comprises four plots registered as L.R.  Nos.  10426/36,  37,  38  &  39  and  is  located  outside  Athi  River town in Machakos District. The site lies about 0.7 km off Mombasa Road, opposite Athi River Steel Plant Limited.

texts.orig: NOTICE is given under the Disposal of Uncollected Goods Act (Cap. 38 ) of the Laws of Kenya to the owners of all items brought to us  for  repairs  including  televisions,  VCRs,  VCPs,  Hi-Fis,  DVDs, radios, inverters, charge controllers e.t.c. on or before 31st July, 2009 and  are  still  lying  with  Television  Sales  and  Rentals  Limited,  to collect them upon payment of repair charges within twenty-one (21) days from the date of publicatiton of this notice. Failure to collect the said  goods  within  this  period  shall  result  in  the  said  goods  being disposed off or sold to recover repair and storage charges without any further notice whatsoever, and no further claims shall be entertained for recovery of such goods thereafter.

texts.text: IMPORTANT NOTICE TO SUBSCRIBERS TO THE KENYA GAZETTE

texts.label: text
texts.orig: THE following notes are for the guidance of persons submitting 'copy' for inclusion in the Kenya Gazette, Supplement , etc.:
texts.text: THE following notes are for the guidance of persons submitting 'copy' for inclusion in the Kenya Gazette, Supplement , etc.:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (1) The Kenya Gazette contains Notices of a general nature which do not affect  legislation.  They  are,  therefore,  submitted  to  the  Government Printer directly.
texts.text: The Kenya Gazette contains Notices of a general nature which do not affect  legislation.  They  are,  therefore,  submitted  to  the  Government Printer directly.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (2) Legislative  Supplement contains  Rules  and  Regulations  which  are issued  by  the  Central  Government.  Because  of  this,  they  must  be submitted  to  the  Government  Printer  through  the  office  of  the Attorney-General.
texts.text: Legislative  Supplement contains  Rules  and  Regulations  which  are issued  by  the  Central  Government.  Because  of  this,  they  must  be submitted  to  the  Government  Printer  through  the  office  of  the Attorney-General.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (3) Bill  Supplement contains  Bills  which  are  for  introduction  in  the National Assembly.
texts.text: Bill  Supplement contains  Bills  which  are  for  introduction  in  the National Assembly.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (4) Act Supplement contains Acts passed by the National Assembly.
texts.text: Act Supplement contains Acts passed by the National Assembly.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All 'copy' submitted for publication should be prepared on one side of an A4  sheet  no  matter  how  small  the  Notice  is,  each  page  being  numbered  and should be typewritten with double spacing. Copy should be clear, legible and contain no alterations.
texts.text: All 'copy' submitted for publication should be prepared on one side of an A4  sheet  no  matter  how  small  the  Notice  is,  each  page  being  numbered  and should be typewritten with double spacing. Copy should be clear, legible and contain no alterations.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Particular attention should be paid to the following points:
texts.text: Particular attention should be paid to the following points:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (i) Signature must be supported by rubber-stamping or typing the name of the signatory in capital letters.
texts.text: (i) Signature must be supported by rubber-stamping or typing the name of the signatory in capital letters.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (ii) Dates must be correct and filled in where necessary.
texts.text: (ii) Dates must be correct and filled in where necessary.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (iii) Care  should  be  taken  to  ensure  that  all  headings  to  Notices  and references to legislation are up to date and conform with the Revised Edition of the Laws of Kenya.
texts.text: (iii) Care  should  be  taken  to  ensure  that  all  headings  to  Notices  and references to legislation are up to date and conform with the Revised Edition of the Laws of Kenya.

texts.orig: 'D  34.  (1)  Communications  for  the Kenya  Gazette should  reach  the Government  Printer  not  later  than  9  a.m.  on  Friday  of  the  week  before publication is desired. The Government Printer will not publish communications received after that hour until the next subsequent issue of the Gazette.
texts.text: 'D  34.  (1)  Communications  for  the Kenya  Gazette should  reach  the Government  Printer  not  later  than  9  a.m.  on  Friday  of  the  week  before publication is desired. The Government Printer will not publish communications received after that hour until the next subsequent issue of the Gazette.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (2) Ministries will be required to pay for the Kenya Gazette and to meet the cost of advertising in it.
texts.text: Ministries will be required to pay for the Kenya Gazette and to meet the cost of advertising in it.

texts.orig: It  is  emphasized that these notes are for guidance only, but it is requested that  persons  submitting  copy  for  publication  first  satisfy  themselves  that  such copy is complete in every respect.

table_cells.text: GAZETTE NOTICE NO. 12949 THE NATIONAL TOURISM CRISIS MANAGEMENT COMMITTEE APPOINTMENT IT IS notified for information of the public that the Minister for Tourism has appointed a Committee to be known as the National Tourism Crisis Management Committee. The Committee shall comprise of the following persons: Eunice K. Miima -( Chairman),

Jake Grieves-Cook-( Vice-Chairperson), Kipkorir Lagat, Dickson Lesimirdana, J.G. King'ori (Col. Rtd), Mutua P. Nzoka, A. Korrir (Eng.), Jacinta M. Kinyua, Mike Macharia, Bettina Vadera (Dr.), Fred Kaigua, Esther Kamweru, Agatha N. Juma, E.K. Waithaka (Col. Rtd.), Juliet Muthoni Gicheru, Lucy Mbugua, Mercy B. Awori, Beatrice Kung'u, Dick Omondi, Sammy Kibet.

Secretariat: Vincent Bwire, Joseph Outa. The Terms of Reference for the Committee shall be- (a) to create and develop a database for all incidents reported and other vital documentation with regard to such incidents;

(b) to carry out post-crisis analysis in conjunction with relevant institutions and make recommendations to the and other relevant institutions with regard to risk better preparedness and improved crisis management.
Establishment of a Crisis Response Committee:

Further, the Ministry has established a (Crisis Response Committee drawn from the Management Committee comprising the following persons: Kipkorir Lagat, Jake Grieves-Cook, Mike Macharia, Fred Kaigua, Esther Kamweru. The Crisis Response Team shall- (a) receive and collect information on any actual or potential risk; (b) evaluate and assess the available information with regard to the significance and magnitude of the actual and potential risk; (c) manage the communication and dissemination of information to all institutions in timely manner; and (d) assist with co-ordination, monitoring and re-evaluation of the risk response efforts. The Committee shall work closely with the National Disaster Management-Co-ordination Centre and other relevant institutions in the management of all crisis situations. In the performance of its function, the Committee may co-opt additional expertise as may be required from time to time, specialized aspects of its terms of reference. The secretariat of the Committee shall be based at:
Utalii House, 6th Floor P.O. Box 30027-00100 Nairobi Tel. +254 20313010
Duration: The membership of the committee shall be for a period of one (1) year, with effect from the 30th June, 2009. Dated the18th November, 2009.
N. M. BALALA, Minister for Tourism.

table_cells.text: GAZETTE NOTICE NO. 13158 THE ENVIRONMENT MANAGEMENT AND CO-ORDINATION
(No . 8 of 1999) ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT ASSESSMENT STUDY REPORT FOR THE PROPOSED PROSPECTING AND MINING OF MANGANESE ORE AT GANZE, KILIFI DISTRICT
PURSUANT to regulation 21 of Environmental (Impact and Audit) Regulations, the National Environment Authority (NEMA) has received an environmental
Assessment Management impact assessment study report for the implementation of the
proposed prospecting and mining of manganese ore at Ganze, Kilifi District. The proposed
project entails prospecting, mining and sale of

manganese (Mn) ore. The project will be located in Ganze Location, Ganze Division, Kilifi District in Coast Province of Kenya and covers about 15 sq.km. The project anticipates the following impacts and mitigation
measures:- Possible Impacts Vegetation loss and Soil erosion
Mitigation Measures  PRANAAV will undertake Alovera and adaptive grass planting as the construction progresses to avoid the soil being washed or carried by wind.
 Places of least vegetation cover will be identified for the campsite and only construction points will be completely cleared to ensure minimum vegetative disturbance.
 Proper roof catchments and gutters will be put in place to control the possible increased runoff.  Temporary storage tanks will be supplied to trap this water for irrigating planted
 The Proponent will do landscaping, and fill the depressions including borrow pits and disused quarries.
 Pumping of water into suitably designed drainage outlets into the seasonal stream will be ensured.
 The quality of the discharged water will be monitored in keeping with the Legal Notice 120, of 2006.
Air pollution from Dust and Engine exhaust gases
 Prompt compaction of loose soils and aggressive grass replanting will be implemented.
 The distance to the neared population concentration is about 1.5km. the impact of the dust on the surrounding people is therefore unlikely.
 Water will be sprinkled on to the disturbed off-pit soil to reduce flying dust.

 Dust screens will be erected to contain flying dust and employees/ construction workers will be provided with personal protective equipment (PPE), to reduce possible dust and noxious gas inhalation.
 Public movement through this mining spots will generally be restricted.
 Emergency responses which might be hindered by the obstruction will be provided for by clear operating procedures.
 Most of the excavated materials will be used for fill-ups and some will be applied in landscaping.
 The staff will not be residing on site but appropriate
 sized potable septic tanks will be availed to staff during their working hours and to keep with the movement of operations.
 Quality standards will be adhered to through initial testing and periodic monitoring in line with the requirements of NEMA contained
 Appropriate soakpits system will be constructed.  The proponent will ensure that it enlists a licensed private company specialized in the
handling oil and solid waste wherever necessary.  Biodegradable kitchen waste will
be composted on site for tree planting manure.  A Good Housekeeping as part of the

company policy will be implemented at the facility.  The mining operations may stop temporarily during the very wet season when working the mines becomes difficult and dangerous.  The project will have an adequate drainage,
on site containment to minimise uncontrolled storm water.  Well documented procedures for maintenance of the drainage system will be implemented and staff will be trained
accordingly.  Oil waste will never be allowed into the environment; proper containment and accidental spill absorption regime will be implemented at all time.
